task_completion.description: The agent successfully loaded the chest CT scan into the medical imaging software (e.g., 3D Slicer).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application window (likely 3D Slicer) is open and displays a loaded CT volume (grayscale anatomical images) in the 2D slice viewers.

task_completion.description: The agent navigated to an axial slice showing the trachea as a distinct, dark, air-filled circular structure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The axial (Red) slice viewer shows a cross-section of the chest with the trachea visible as a black circular hole anterior to the spine.

task_completion.description: The agent selected a ruler or markup tool to perform the measurement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The mouse cursor is selecting a ruler/line tool from the toolbar, or the 'Markups' module is active in the sidebar.

task_completion.description: The agent drew a measurement line across the lumen of the trachea.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible line (markup) is drawn spanning the diameter of the dark tracheal circle in one of the slice viewers.

task_completion.description: The measured diameter displayed on the screen is accurate relative to the ground truth (approx 18.0 mm).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The label next to the measurement line reads a value between 16.0 mm and 20.0 mm (Ground Truth is 18.0 mm).

task_completion.description: The agent generated and saved the JSON report containing the measurement and ETT recommendation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file preview window is visible showing a JSON structure with keys like 'tracheal_diameter_mm' and 'recommended_ett_size_mm'.

integrity.description: The agent used the software GUI to measure, rather than estimating or using terminal scripts exclusively.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual medical imaging interface with the measurement markup drawn on the image, not just a terminal window.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected answer without performing the measurement.
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement value appears as a dynamic label on the image ruler tool before appearing in the final report.

integrity.description: The reported measurement matches the visual ruler on screen.
integrity.visual_evidence: The value seen in the JSON report matches the value displayed on the ruler in the slice viewer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset is synthetic with a hardcoded tracheal diameter of exactly 18.0 mm. The VLM should verify that the measurement tool on screen shows a value close to 18.0 mm (acceptable range 16.0-20.0 mm). The ETT recommendation logic provided in the task (OD = ID + 2mm; OD = Trachea - 2to3mm) implies a target ID of roughly 13-14mm, which is larger than standard sizes (max 8.5mm). If the agent recommends the max standard size (8.5mm) or the calculated size, either is acceptable as long as the diameter measurement of ~18mm is correct.
